compiled_state.title: Remove v2 ticket system references (tk command and .tickets/ directory)
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Context
Agents reading the codebase encounter dead v2 ticket system code — dual-path conditionals, tk command calls, .tickets/ path patterns, and the issue_tracker.create_cmd config key — left over from the v2-to-v3 migration. These references increase agent investigation time (agents must determine which code path is active), inflate context windows with irrelevant branches, and create confusion when docs reference both tk and ticket CLIs for the same operations.

## Success Criteria
- No references to the tk command or .tickets/ directory remain in scripts, hooks, skills, config, CI workflows, examples, or docs (verified by grep returning zero matches in non-ADR files)
- No issue_tracker.create_cmd config key or its associated dead code exists anywhere in the codebase
- All dual-path v2/v3 conditionals in scripts are collapsed to v3-only code paths
- ROLLBACK-PROCEDURE.md is deleted
- All existing tests pass after v2 removal; test files that exclusively test v2 behavior are removed, and tests with v2 fallback assertions are updated to v3-only
- Post-cleanup, an agent asked to trace how a ticket is created encounters only v3 code paths with no v2 branches or ambiguity (validated by dogfooding the cleanup against a ticket-creation trace)

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Single-pass cleanup epic with stories grouped by file category (scripts, hooks, docs, tests, CI/config). Each story removes all v2 references in its category and updates corresponding tests.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Epic complete: all 6 stories closed, validation score 5/5
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
